title: lichen_l2_iface_init sets lichen_iface before checking lichen_log_link_local_from_eui64
description:
lichen_l2.c:461-467: The code sets lichen_iface = iface at line 461, then calls lichen_log_link_local_from_eui64() at line 467. If logging fails, goto fail_late_init clears the callback but does NOT clear lichen_iface (per the invariant comment).

What goes wrong: After fail_late_init, lichen_iface is non-NULL and iface_init_failed is set. The lora_rx_callback at line 392 checks both conditions:

    if (lichen_iface == NULL || atomic_get(&iface_init_failed))

This correctly rejects the packet. So the logic is sound.

However, the invariant comment at line 85-88 says 'Set once, never cleared'. But what if iface_init is called twice? Zephyr shouldn't do this, but if it did, lichen_iface would be overwritten. There's no guard.

Actually, NET_DEVICE_INIT creates the net_if statically and init is called once. This is a theoretical concern, not a real bug.

Cancel - the existing guards are sufficient.
